title: We Can Learn to Talk Again
subtitle: A three-minute affirmation for the long partnership where the talking has gone quiet
excerpt: A three-minute affirmation for the long partnership where the talking has gone quiet. The affirmation does not promise everything will be easy. It does keep the door of the conversation open.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The we-can-learn-again affirmation shape is common in writing on long-term partnership.

content.text: A three-minute affirmation for the long partnership where the talking has gone quiet. Said three times, slow, hand on the heart.
New to affirmation as a practice? Read

Long partnerships go quiet at points. The reasons are usually small. A few hard months stack up. The work was busy. The babies were small. The grief was loud. The habit of talking just stopped being daily.
The affirmation is not a promise that the talking will return on its own. It is a quiet refusal to write the partnership off because the talking has gone quiet.
We can learn to talk again. The door is still open. I am ready to walk through it slowly.
Sit somewhere quiet. Hand on the heart. Say the affirmation three times, slow. The third time can be the slowest.
Use it in the morning of a planned talk, or in the day after one that did not land. The affirmation holds the door; the day's small move is the walking through it.
Original to homemade.education. The we-can-learn-again shape is a common one. It appears in many books on long partnership.
